item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: SCP Foundation Wiki
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Title: 1872-new.jpg
Author: Roget
Release year: 2020

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one a lot but I'd like a bit more on its recovery/origin especially if the user died/was killed during recovery, I just want to know what happened there if it's not a classic "we took it and mind wiped em"

item.title: Re: STAFF POST- OPEN CROSSLINKING
item.content:encoded: I take slight issue (as the author of 3104 and 555) with the crosslink to 555, as 555 is a purely mechanical effect; this thing doesn't draw broken-down cars toward it.

item.title: STAFF POST- OPEN CROSSLINKING
item.content:encoded:
The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

Show reasoning
Hide reasoning

The following crosslinks are being connected to this article due to the similarity in effect IE seeming attraction of certain objects/items to a specific source.

Editing this:

The persons who were found with SCP-1872 were killed during recovery operations. SCP-1872 was classified as Safe as of ██/██/2009.

To this:

The persons who were found with SCP-1872 were killed during recovery operations. SCP-1872 was classified as Safe as of ██/██/2009.

Despite no other connections, SCP-1872 has remarkably similar effects to SCP-3104 and SCP-555, although in a far more directed and controllable manner. Further research into these anomalies may provide insight into how SCP-1872 functions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Think "laze", I assume.

Edit: No, I lied. Is probably a joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Um, if I'm reading the wiktionary right where it says lazer is a common misspelling of laser, please hear my plea once more

If there's something I'm missing going on here, then shame on me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My head canon for this scp is that heelys are vehicles. Just imagine it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This locker is to be checked once a day for signs of tampering.

This skip does nothing that would tamper the locker because if it's off, no vehicles are coming towards it. Unless you mean "some staff might take it for giggles", which is weird. We'd assume that security cameras or at least these lockers being in a secure location would prevent anyone stealing it. This line means nothing.

SCP-1872 was classified as Safe as of ██/██/2009.

Also seems extraneous.

Despite that… I'm a sucker for this. Making vehicles act like cats, and the vehicles wanting to keep playing with the lasers, inadvertently injuring the user if they don't keep it up… that's cute, with the unfortunate side-effect of ending in tragedy when things go horribly wrong. But still… cat vehicle!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yep:
l,a,z,e,r.

"I was playing Mortal Kombat at the LazerTag place."

Light Amplification by Zapping Electromagnetic Radiation. ;p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know what you're talking about, 'lazer' is totes the correct spelling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved it - simply and cleanly executed with right amount of anomalousness (i know that's not a word).

But,

If SCP-1872's light is directed within 1.5 meters of any vehicle with a person inside of it, the vehicle will begin to actively pursue the light.

&

with testing having shown it can affect at least up to 37 automobiles, 5 aquatic vehicles, 4 helicopters, 12 bicycles, and 2 trains.

I find it hard to believe with such a narrow range of effect it actually attracted 60 vehicles at once.

One more thing, the title of this SCP is spelled "lazer" pointer; I don't know if that was intentional because inside the article it is actually written "laser" which is correct spelling - if not, couldn't hurt to fix the typo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If these vehicles catch up to the user, they will begin to slowly approach the user, blinking their headlights and revving their engines.

That's…adorable :3c
+1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you could get a space station/spaceship within range of the laser, then yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does it affect spacecraft and space stations?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had this hilarious visual in my head of cars chasing this laser, undercarriages arched, sticking their radio antennae up in the air and shifting their weight from one back tire to the other like some sort of domesticated car cat.

Car… cat… ohhh, NOW I get that pun from earlier in the comments.

*shoots that commenter and upboats* Great article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've made more edits of that nature

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I see a picture of a fuck-all-huge pileup, caused by an SCP, and my mind goes to work. Magnets? Gravity? Spacial distortion?

… A laser that suddenly gives vehicles the mentality of cats.

My funny-bone just got sucker-punched, well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. I never said motor vehicle, just vehicle. I count bikes and such as vehicles.

2. It keep trying to get by until it destroys itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Skateboards and bicycles aren't really vehicles. (unless it had a motor or something) So I fail to see why it would affect such things. Also, what happens if the machine is blocked by something I simply can't go any further?

Fix this stuff and I'm definitely giving +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think Mann's recommendation retains the playful tone a bit better, but the horn works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seeing as how the universe would clearly be turned inside out, I'd call that a ZK.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah yes, I had forgotten. Thank you for reminding me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unfortunately, that's not coming across in the article. Like I suggested in chat, I think it will work better if you ramp it up gradually. Have them bump the user a few times, then hit them a little harder. They're not just trying to kill the user, they're trying to get them to use the laser again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It isn't. The cars want you to keep the lazer going, and get frustrated if you don't keep going.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the sticking point for me too. I love the idea, but this feels like lethality for lethality's sake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I cracked the fuck up when I pictured this, and then shuddered when I thought about how it would ultimately end. That is what I want in an SCP. +1, no regrets.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you have the right of it. And I was watching Maximum Overdrive yesterday.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I laughed a lot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it a lot. I only have one concern, the vehicles killing the person seems a bit tacked on. But a +1 none-the-less.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're welcome ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is good. It could stand to have many of the sentences tightened up, but it's good.

It weighs 120 grams when it has no batteries, and is exactly 25 millimeters long

It's only an inch long? That's going to be a bitch to aim. Standard laser pointers are more like 150 mm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Idea is cute, but execution feels clunky. I'll try to address specific issues tomorrow when I'm more conscious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This locker is to be checked once a day for signs of tampering.

There doesn't seem to be anything about the SCP that would require this sort of checking.

The light produced by SCP-1872 is approximately 650 nm, despite this SCP-1872 appears to have a range of at least 1.5 kilometers.

Wait, so red light at that wavelength shouldn't be capable of travelling that far?

No personnel are to be present inside of these vehicles during testing.

If SCP-1872's light is directed within 1.5 meters any vehicle with a person inside of it,

Should the containment say something like "Only D-class personnel are to be present inside …"?

If the light produced by SCP-1872 is still, vehicles will circle it until it begins to move again.

What if the light is projected directly onto a vehicle?

SCP-1872 appears to affect any vehicle that a person is capable of operating, and has been found to be able to affect a variety of vehicles, including (but not limited to) vehicles such as automobiles, bicycles, skateboards, construction equipment, aircrafts, and aquatic vessels.

With things like bicycles and skateboards, does the vehicle move itself, or is the body of the person on it taken over in order to power the vehicle? If they move by themselves, then why do vehicles like cars stop moving when they run out of fuel?

and 2 trains.

Does the SCP cause the trains to jump off the tracks and move without the use of tracks, or does the train stay on the tracks and stop moving when it gets as close as possible to the light?

However, if the user deactivates SCP-1872 before the vehicles that have been affected by it are incapacitated, they will begin to pursue the user until SCP-1872 is reactivated.

What if the user points the SCP up into the sky? Does that count as deactivation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember seeing that picture a while ago, I`m glad someone could make an SCP out of it.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is terrible and you should feel terrible.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really don't know what to think of this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dr. Vantas does not approve of this comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: XK

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If they can get the sub in a position where it would be affected it would.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Last question: does it affect submarines?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It turns them into… karkats!

/shot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hah…not bad. Very catty of you. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It affects all sorts of planes, trains, and automobiles. And other stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does this only apply to vehicles or will it affect aircraft, trains, off-road utility vehicles, motorcycles, boats, etc? Also will it affect AC cars or miniatures?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if you're in a car when you use it?
